entry.title: SAP Build Apps - OData Integration – Customize CSRF Token Fetching URL
entry.content:
In this blog, I will discuss the recent enhancement in SAP Build Apps for CSRF token handling, specifically on how to customize where to fetch the CSRF token for OData integration. You may already know that there is an additional property in SAP BTP Destination dedicated to SAP Build Apps CSRF token handling:

 

BuildApps.RequiresCsrf = true

 

If your destination requires the CSRF token to be passed, this property needs to be set to true. To learn more about this, please check out the blog

Understand CSRF handling in SAP Build Apps.

Now, let's get back to the newly released enhancement. We have introduced a new property, 'CSRF Token,' in the General Settings section under the Data Configuration tab in the App Editor for OData integration with SAP systems. As shown in the screenshot below, you can configure where to fetch the CSRF token from your backend SAP system. By default, the 'CSRF Token' property is disabled

It will be enabled after you set the additional property BuildApps.RequiresCsrf = true in the BTP destination and re-open the App Editor, as shown in the screenshot below

 

You can now specify where to fetch the CSRF token from your backend OData service. Once you tick the checkbox 'Overwrite CSRF Token URL',  the default value in the 'CSRF Token URL' input box is a slash /, which means to fetch the CSRF token from the root folder of your backend OData service. For example, if your BTP destination ‘MyODataDest’ setting is as shown below

 

#

#Thu Sep 05 07:22:58 UTC 2024

Type=HTTP

BuildApps.RequiresCsrf=true

Name=MyODataDest

URL=https\://mycompany.com/OData/myodatasvc

ProxyType=Internet

AppgyverEnabled=true

 

SAP Build Apps runtime will use HTTP HEAD and GET requests to fetch a CSRF token from https://mycompany.com/OData/myodatasvc. This token is then used for subsequent HTTP POST, PUT, PATCH, or DELETE requests.

If the root folder of your backend OData service does not support HTTP HEAD and GET requests for fetching the CSRF token, you need to customize the default value '/' to a relative path that does. For example, you can set the CSRF token URL to '/path/svc1'.

In this case, the SAP Build Apps runtime, which means the deployed app to SAP Build Work Zone or the standalone mobile app running on iOS and Android, will fetch the CSRF token from https://mycompany.com/OData/myodatasvc/path/svc1 and use it for later HTTP POST, PUT, or DELETE requests against https://mycompany.com/OData/myodatasvc.

entry.title: Driving Innovation with SAP Build at SAP TechEd 2024
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 delivered a series of transformative announcements, setting the stage for how developers and businesses will innovate using SAP technology. With a strong focus on generative AI and advancements in SAP Build, this year's event underscored SAP's commitment to driving next-generation business transformation.

Here is a comprehensive look at the major highlights for SAP Build from this year's SAP TechEd.

 

SAP Build: Streamlining Development Across the Board

One of the most significant advancements from SAP TechEd 2024 is the expansion of SAP Build. SAP Build enables developers to extend and create applications on SAP S/4HANA Cloud and other line-of-business applications seamlessly. SAP's new extensibility wizard allows developers to create extensions from S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with SAP Build, while maintaining critical business context like objects, events, and data. This feature ensures that extensions, whether developed side-by-side or on-stack, preserve essential business logic without data loss.

Developers will also be able to leverage the extensibility wizard to create SAP Fiori and SAPUI5 applications directly within SAP Build, reducing time-to-market for new apps and simplifying workflows.

Last but certainly not least, SAP announced the integration of ABAP development environments within SAP Build. By the end of 2024, developers will be able to access ABAP tools from the SAP Build Lobby, the platform's central hub for all development projects. This update makes SAP Build a unified development environment, allowing fusion teams to collaborate seamlessly across multiple technology stacks. Teams can now move effortlessly between SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Code, and ABAP environments, driving faster innovation.

For a more comprehensive view of what this looks like for developers, click here.

 

Generative AI: Joule and Beyond

Generative AI was a central theme through SAP TechEd 2024 with new capabilities designed to make developers' lives easier and applications smarter. Joule, SAP's generative AI assistant, is set to expand its capabilities across the SAP Build portfolio, providing AI-powered task automation, intelligent support within development environments, and more.

In 2025, ABAP developers will gain the ability to use Joule to generate high-quality code and unit tests that adhere to ABAP Cloud development standards. Joule will also assist with legacy code explanations, helping developers modernize outdated systems and accelerate the transition to clean core.

Developers can also look forward to Joule Studio, a new platform for building custom AI skills for Joule. These skills can be integrated into S/4HANA Cloud, allowing businesses to create bespoke AI-powered workflows tailored to their specific needs. Joule's generative AI capabilities will soon extend beyond SAP environments, allowing developers to integrate non-SAP applications with ease.

SAP also introduced new features in the Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core, with models like IBM Granite, DALL·E 3, and Amazon Titan, coming to the platform by the end of 2024. These tools will empower developers to create AI-driven applications, including image generation and sophisticated AI orchestration workflows. Generative AI Hub will also offer prompt templates, data masking, and orchestration capabilities to streamline AI implementation across various use cases. 

 

What SAP TechEd 2024 Means for Developers

SAP TechEd 2024 showcased a clear vision for the future, with innovations in SAP Build and generative AI that will reshape how developers and businesses approach enterprise applications. With the continuous expansion of AI-driven features like Joule and the unified development environments, SAP is positioning itself at the forefront of digital transformation.

As we move into 2025, developers can expect even more opportunities to leverage coding capabilities of all skillsets and AI to create smarter, more efficient business solutions. Whether you're a seasoned developer or just beginning your journey with SAP, the new tools and enhancements announced at TechEd will empower you to build the future of enterprise technology.

Start exploring these powerful new capabilities today by signing up for an SAP BTP Trial account and take your first step toward building the future of enterprise applications.

entry.title: GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base: An Overview of the base edition bundle
entry.content:
GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base: An Overview of the base edition

Since March 2023, customers who have selected SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as the core ERP system to run their businesses have received additional entitlements beyond the core functionality. These added capabilities form one of two comprehensive bundles: GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base and GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, premium.
 
In this blog, I will focus on the base edition bundle, providing a clear explanation of each entitlement and its business value. I will discuss the premium edition in a separate blog, which I will hyperlink here once created.

This blog is designed for business users and consultants with ERP experience, aiming to provide practical insights into each entitlement's purpose and when it’s most valuable. Please note, statements about adoption difficulty and required personas are based on my research and experience, and other cases may vary based on the specific business context etc.
Note 1: Hyperlinks to SAP4ME will take you directly to the relevant page if you're logged into a productive SAP4ME system and have suitable rights
Note 2: This blog speaks to the entitlements included as of 2024-10-17

Blog Outline:

  1. GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Base: Overview
  2. Core Entitlement: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  3. General Notes on Additional Entitlements
  4. Base Entitlement: Finance - Group Reporting
  5. Base Entitlement: BTP - SAP Build Process Automation
  6. Base Entitlement: BTP - SAP Build Workzone, Standard Edition
  7. Base Entitlement: BTP - CPEA Credits
  8. Base Entitlement: SAP Digital Access
  9. Base Entitlement: Ariba Buying, Base Edition
  10. Closing Remarks

 

  1. GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Base: Overview
    The Base Edition comes in two SKUs: 8014771 for global markets and 8014782 for the Greater China region (which excludes some entitlements). This blog focuses on SKU 8014771.

    How to identify if you have this SKU:
    1. Log in to SAP for Me: Reporting > Purchased Solutions
    2. Under 'Orders by Solution Area,' select S/4HANA Public - your SKU will be listed on the right. If 8014771 appears with a valid date, you have the GROW Base Edition. If 8014761 appears, you have Premium. Other SKUs likely represent SKUs that do not include this full suite of entitlements.

  2. Core Entitlement: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    The core entitlement includes:
    1. Services & Support: Access to support services like incident creation, onboarding advisor support, and the SAP Enterprise Support Portfolio, which offers learning content and services such as Continuous Quality Checks.
    2. Systems: Access to multiple systems (e.g., ERP production, test, and development systems) and related tenants. This also includes non-ERP systems like Cloud ALM, CBC, SAP for Me, and BTP systems.
    3. Functionality: Access to pre-configured scope items. Commonly used ones include J59 - Accounts Receivable and J60 - Accounts Payable. You can view and manage scope items through SAP Activate’s Availability and Dependencies of Scope Items accelerator.
    4. Full User Equivalent (FUE): Your contract includes a specific number of FUEs that govern how many business users can access core functionality. Check your licensed FUE quantity in SAP for Me: Reporting - License Consumption under metric CY334.
    5. Entitlement Measurement: Overuse is possible but monitored by SAP. Your account team will reach out in case of overconsumption.

  3. General Notes on Additional Entitlements
    What is an "entitlement"?
    An entitlement refers to the right to use a specific set of functionalities that normally requires additional licensing (at a cost). In the GROW Base Edition, certain entitlements are included up to a threshold without extra software licensing costs. For example, get an entitlement to use up to 25 objects with the Group Reporting Functionality - this blog goes through entitlements like this which are included in your bundle.

    Are the "entitlements" completely free?
    The outlined entitlements mean there's no software licensing cost if you use up to the amount indicated. Usage beyond that quantity will require additional (paid) licenses, and you may need to consider some investment of business time and effort to adopt new features and functions, and in some cases you'll require consultants for the setup and implementation.

    What does "adoption" mean in this blog?
    I'm using 'adoption' to refer to the setup AND usage a software functionality. The setup or implementation is the process of adopting, and once it's setup and being actively used  - then I would then say the customer has adopted a feature/entitlement.
    Base Edition - Additional Entitlements:
    GROW with SAP Public Edition, base - additional entitlements
  4. Base Edition Entitlement: Group Reporting
    Business Case
    Group Reporting helps businesses that operate multiple subsidiaries or legal entities shorten the time needed for financial consolidation. It automates key activities and streamlines the data model, making it highly valuable for businesses with complex financial structures. For single-entity businesses, this functionality is not immediately relevant.

    Business Role Relevance
    Senior finance roles, such as Finance Managers and Group Controllers, will benefit from this functionality for managing financial consolidations.

    Entitlement
    The GROW Base Edition includes 25 Group Reporting objects. An object typically refers to a legal entity within a consolidation group (e.g., parent and subsidiary companies). Additional licenses are required for more than 25 objects. Official documentation is here

    License Consumption
    You can track your consumption using metric CH112 in SAP for Me under SAP4ME: Reporting -> License Consumption.

    Overusage
    It is possible to over-consume your allocated Group Reporting objects. SAP will periodically review your usage under metric CH112. You can license additional units by contacting SAP for SKU 8013710.

    Scope Item Relevance
    The main scope item for Group Reporting is 1SG. This measures whether data from group reporting has been posted into the system. You can validate its usage in Portfolio & Products - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Usage. Other Scope Items flagged as Group Reporting relevant are  287, 28B, 4R7, 2U6

    More Information
    For more detailed guidance on adopting Group Reporting, refer to this blog post. This deeper descriptions, webinar links, and crucially it outlines the key steps to adopting group reporting

    Key Adoption Notes:
    Expertise needed: You'll need a combination of BTP administrators and finance consultants for configuration and initial setup.
    Adoption Effort:  Rated 3 (on a scale from 1 to 5). You’ll need finance consultants, and technical consultants with expertise in BTP to set up this functionality.
    Not to be confused with: This entitlement does not include Group Reporting Data Collection, which must be licensed separately.

  5. Base Edition Entitlement: SAP Build Process Automation
    Business Case
    SAP Build Process Automation enables non-developers, such as key users or business process experts, to create bots or automated workflows based on a set of rules. This is valuable when no standard ERP functionality exists to meet a specific business need, for example, automating the creation of general ledger accounts.

    Business Role Relevance
    Consultants, process automation specialists, and business key users involved in implementing and running the ERP system will find this useful.

    Entitlement
    The base edition includes:
    20 Standard Users (CP278) – Can trigger or interact with a process.
    2 Advanced Users (CP279) – Can design, monitor, and administrate processes.
    1 Unattended Automation (CP283) – A bot that continuously monitors and executes a process.
    Documentation of these entitlements are here

    License Consumption
    You can track your consumption of CP278, CP279, and CP283 in SAP4ME - Reporting - License

    Overusage
    You are blocked from overconsuming these licenses. Once the entitlement limit is reached, additional licenses must be purchased or allocated through CPEA credits.

    Scope Item Relevance
    There is no specific scope item for SAP Build Process Automation.

    More Information
    This community page has overview videos and links to all other resources. This area of the API hub is where you find SAP pre-created templates.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: You’ll need a BTP expert for the base setup. Then you'll need LoB experts e.g. finance experts to develop meaningful automations as per your needs. Setup steps are outlined here.
    Adoption Effort: 2/5 for platform setup by BTP experts; 2-4/5 for workflow creation depending on complexity.

  6. Base Edition Entitlement: SAP Build Workzone, Standard Edition

    Business Case
    SAP Build Workzone allows organizations to create a central homepage for employees with user-specific access to applications across various SAP systems. This enhances efficiency by providing direct links to necessary business apps for roles like sales managers or purchasing clerks. The more SAP systems used throughout your organization, the more value this solution will offer your organization.

    Business Role Relevance
    This functionality benefits employees across departments who frequently use SAP applications in their daily tasks.

    Entitlement
    You receive 1 Workzone user per FUE licensed. For example, if you have licensed 100 FUEs for the core, 100 users can access SAP Build Workzone.

    License Consumption
    You can track your consumption of CP264 in SAP4ME - Reporting - License Consumption

    Overusage
    Additional licenses or CPEA credits are required if the number of users exceeds your entitlement.

    Scope Item Relevance
    There is no scope item related to SAP Build Workzone usage.

    More Information
    More documentation is on the SAP Build Workzone Discovery Center.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: A BTP global administrator for initial setup, followed by non-technical key users for managing pages.
    Adoption Effort: 2/5 for BTP experts; 3/5 for non-experts. View the setup steps in this tutorial.
    Not to be confused with: SAP Build Workzone is distinct from SAP Start, which a more lightweight solution without ability to consider other SAP system authorizations. SAP Build Workzone Advanced Edition is a different product without a migration path from the standard edition.

  7. Base Edition Entitlement: CPEA Credits

    Business Case
    CPEA Credits (Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement) allow businesses to consume various BTP services within predefined limits without additional fees. These credits can be applied to services like Integration Suite, SAP Build Apps, and Cloud Identity Services. You can view the list of included services here in the Discovery Center. There's more nuance to the CPEA credit commercial model, which you can read about here.

    Business Role Relevance
    Technical teams managing BTP services will oversee consumption of this entitlement. The business areas that consume this depend on which services you're utilizing from BTP - many are in the technical / administration area.

    Entitlement
    Customers receive 1% of their net Annual Contract Value in CPEA credits, with a minimum value of €2,000 and a maximum value of €16,000.

    License Consumption
    You can view your CPEA credit balance in SAP4ME - Finance and Legal - Enterprise Agreements

    Scope Item Relevance
    CPEA credit usage is not tied to any scope item, as it applies across various BTP services.

    More Information
    Each service that can be paid for by CPEA credits are listed here in the Discovery Center - and this same resource provides additional information on each service.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: BTP experts and business area owners are required to manage and utilize CPEA credits effectively.
    Adoption Effort: Varies depending on the BTP service being used.
    Not to be confused with: CPEA credits differ from BTPEA and EA credits, which operate similarly but cover different services.

  8. Base Edition Entitlement: SAP Digital Access

    Business Case
    SAP Digital Access governs how non-human interactions (such as external systems creating documents) are licensed. It is relevant when integrating third-party systems like Salesforce to trigger document creation within SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

    Business Role Relevance
    IT and integration specialists responsible for system integrations with non-SAP platforms will need to manage this entitlement.

    Entitlement
    The base edition includes 100,000 digital access documents per year.

    License Consumption
    Track your consumption by launching the License Compliance Digital Access app in the productive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system. This guidance document shows how to do this and provides other useful context on measuring your digital access usage for SAP S/4HANA

    Overusage
    It is possible to exceed your annual document allowance. SAP periodically reviews your consumption and the account team will reach out in the case of overconsumption.

    Scope Item Relevance
    This entitlement applies to external system interactions and is not tied to specific scope items.

    More Information
    Refer to the Digital Access documentation for a more detailed explanation.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: No specific competency is needed. Entitlement consumption should be reviewed by the party responsible for your SAP system billing & commercials.
    Adoption Effort: 1. There's nothing to setup per se for Digital Access - you only be aware of it and monitor your consumption.
    Other Notes: SAP Digital Access is only necessary for non-SAP systems. If SAP systems trigger document creation, digital access is not consumed.

  9. Base Edition Entitlement: Ariba Buying, Base Edition

    Business Case
    Ariba Buying Base Edition allows employees to make internal purchase requisitions. It's scope is similar to the core ERP requisitioning scope of 18J, but Ariba Buying Base has an enhanced UX and has the capability to integrate with many advanced features, such as directly connecting to supplier catalogs and the Ariba Business network.

    Business Role Relevance
    All company employees can benefit from an improved user experience for purchase requisitions, particularly in organizations using self-service requisitioning.

    Entitlement
    There is no access limit for the base functionality, but additional licensing may be required for extended capabilities, such as linking to external supplier catalogs.

    License Consumption
    There are no limits to usage of this entitlement. It operates more like a standard scope item, but many of the functionalities that bring Ariba Buying Base to life will require additional licenses.

    Scope Item Relevance
    Scope item 4AI measures whether you've setup the communications arrangement required to use Ariba Buying base edition.

    More Information
    View an overview of Ariba Buying in this video demo - but note this video includes the utilization of catalogs that may require additional licensing.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: BTP administrators and procurement experts are required for setup and catalog integration.
    Adoption Effort: Unclear
    Other Notes: A lot of the functionality’s value comes from adding catalogs and integrating with the broader Ariba Network, which may require additional licensing.

  10. Closing
    The intent of this blog was to give you a base understanding of the each entitlement in the base edition - what it is, and when it's most valuable.
    Any feedback regarding on the content including other use cases, other resources to link etc. are welcome and I'll periodically update this blog with improved information.

entry.title: Announcing the New "In-Metro" Disaster Recovery Solution for SAP BTP
entry.content:
Overview

We’re excited to unveil the latest enhancement to SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP):
The In-Metro Disaster Recovery (DR) solution.

This solution is designed to protect you against disasters affecting a single Availability Zone (AZ) with contractually committed SLAs RPO and RTO, offering enhanced resilience and uninterrupted operations for your cloud services.

 

What is "In-Metro" DR for SAP BTP?

The In-Metro DR solution is built to protect against local disasters by leveraging a Multi-AZ setup. It ensures that, in the event of a single AZ disaster, which is defined by SAP, services remain operational, data is protected, and business continues without significant downtime.

The key metrics are:

  • RPO (Recovery Point Objective): 5 minutes – Ensuring that data can be recovered with a maximum of 5 minutes' loss in case of a disaster.
  • RTO (Recovery Time Objective): 2 hours – Ensuring that the full system is restored and operational within 2 hours of an incident.

 

The Power of Multi-AZ Architecture

At the heart of In-Metro DR is the Multi-AZ (Multi Availability Zone) architecture, ensuring that your applications and databases are spread across different zones to safeguard against single-point failures.

This includes:

  • Active-active applications across 3 AZs: Applications are distributed across three AZs, with real-time data replication ensuring that if one AZ fails, the others automatically take over without impacting availability.
  • Active-passive databases across 2 AZs: Databases are set up in active-passive mode, with a failover mechanism in place to switch operations to a backup AZ if the primary one fails.

Through automatic failover, load balancing, and failure detection, the solution ensures that services stay online seamlessly. Internal stress tests, such as regular “Chaos Day” exercises, further reinforce the reliability of this DR solution.

 

Availability and Scope

 As of now, the In-Metro DR solution is not available for all SAP BTP services. While it already covers a wide range of services, its scope will continue to expand over time as we improve coverage and extend it to additional SAP BTP services. The services in scope will be regularly updated in the corresponding documents.

In-Metro DR is specifically designed for SAP-managed scenarios, meaning that customers using SAP BTP under SAP’s management can take advantage of the In-Metro DR setup without additional configuration or costs. However, for customer-managed scenarios, such as side-by-side extensions, the customer is responsible for configuring it accordingly (e.g. deploy the apps with multiple instances or configure data replicas on the persistency level).

 

 DR Planning and Regular Testing

 To ensure the highest levels of reliability:

  • The DR plan is updated every 12 months, ensuring that it incorporates the latest technology and best practices.
  • A DR test is conducted at least once a year to verify that the solution can meet its RPO and RTO commitments under real-world conditions.

The In-Metro DR solution currently supports SAP BTP regions hosted on both AWS and Azure cloud infrastructures, providing flexibility for your organization’s cloud setup.

 

 Next Steps

 By leveraging In-Metro DR within SAP-managed scenarios, you can trust that your critical data and services are protected with cutting-edge resilience, without the need for extra investment. For customer-managed scenarios, setting up corresponding HA and DR solutions will ensure your setup is equally resilient.

To explore which services are already covered and get all information, check the description in SAP Trust Center.

Alternatively, you can watch a deep dive into this new solution at SAP TechED: Watch the SAP TechED session on In-Metro DR.

entry.title: You ask, we answer - Ask Me Anything Session (Oct 24)
entry.content:
During SAP TechEd 2024, SAP unveiled significant improvements to SAP Build, which included making SAP S/4HANA extension development more efficient, introducing new generative AI features, and providing broader access to ABAP development tools. These changes empower the developer community to innovate more easily and with increased agility. 

You can read all about the announcements in Michael Ameling's blog post: Unveiling today: SAP Build meets ABAP Cloud! And the good news is: you can still access the session catalog to review the content and stay current with the latest innovations & enjoy Devtoberst sessions until October 25, check out the agenda here.

Now if you're curious about what you saw during the event and have some more questions, or you would like to dive deeper, here's a great opportunity:

We'll be hosting a live Ask Me Anything session on October 24, 3pm CET. You can watch it on YouTube, or click the preview above. In the session, the panel of experts will recap the features announced and answer your questions live. 

Can't join? No worries, drop your questions in the comments and the experts will answer them live for you to watch later 😉

Looking forward to seeing you there!

Cheers,
Maiara

entry.title: The next step of ABAPs evolution: ABAP Cloud now part of SAP Build
entry.content:
"We’ve always defined ourselves by the ability to overcome the impossible." (Movie: Interstellar, 2014)

Here we are again, with the next step in the evolution of ABAP.

ABAP Cloud

It’s been six years since SAP launched the so-called SAP Cloud Platform ABAP Environment, which allowed developers to build and run ABAP code in the cloud. Now called SAP BTP ABAP Environment, it is the SAP Platform-as-a-Service offering that enables developers to leverage their traditional ABAP skills to build and run ABAP applications in the cloud on SAP BTP, either as an extension to SAP S/4HANA (any edition) or as standalone applications. With the SAP BTP ABAP environment, we are targeting business application developers who have extensive SAP experience and domain knowledge, particularly around SAP ERP solutions. See Steampunk is going all-in and and It's Steampunk now, to read about it’s history.

Derived from this, something ingenious was introduced: The ABAP Cloud development model. ABAP Cloud is a perfect example of how innovation works. The story of ABAP Cloud began quite silent. In fact, ABAP Cloud was born in the SAP BTP ABAP environment, where  it’s the leading and only development model available for ABAP development. It’s the amazing result of the ABAP engineering team, which has evolved ABAP into ABAP Cloud, making it future-proof, upgrade-stable and, you could say, cloud native. 

With the ABAP Cloud development model, you can build cloud-ready and upgrade-stable business applications, services, and extensions. ABAP Cloud was introduced in the BTP ABAP environment, but like any other innovation success story, ABAP Cloud has expanded its home turf, it has grown in both scope and availability.

That’s how the idea was born to make ABAP Cloud available everywhere.

The result is that ABAP Cloud is already the underlying ABAP development model in SAP BTP ABAP environment and in S/4HANA Cloud, public edition as of 2208 (3 system landscape required). Additionally, it can be used in S/4HANA Cloud, private edition as well as in S/4HANA any premise from >2022. We highly recommend using ABAP Cloud already for your S/4HANA private cloud as well as S/4HANA on premise projects to set the guardrails to reach a clean core and preparing your cloud journey.

 

Picture 1: ABAP Cloud Availability

SAP Build 

Back to the opening statement: the next step in ABAP’s evolution. ABAP Cloud is now part of SAP Build! This is the amazing success story of how true integration works. How technology can overcome the borders of more than 40 years of evolutionary history. For more details on this great enhancement, read this technical blog post by Michael Ameling.

But what happens when the paths of SAP Build and ABAP Cloud cross to form the world’s strongest alliance for customer and partner development in the SAP ecosystem? What does this mean for you, our developer community?

With SAP Build, you can accelerate your ERP modernization, foster innovation and automate processes, all within a single comprehensive suite of extension development tools that includes low-code, pro-code and generative AI capabilities.

SAP Build provides you the flexibility to create and extend applications, automate processes and design digital workspaces with low-code, pro-code and generative AI tools. Its collaborative design allows diverse teams to build solutions faster using pre-built solutions, AI assistance, and seamless integration with SAP applications.​

It has never been easier from a developer’s perspective, since SAP Build provides you all the tools you need in one place,  so you can focus on the real development to be done. The SAP Build Lobby is the starting point for your Java/JavaScript projects and your ABAP Cloud projects.

Picture 2: SAP Build Lobby

Our goal is to provide you with a seamless development flow, regardless of the environment you’re working in most of the time. Examples of this integration include

  • The tool bridge between the ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse (ADT) and SAP Business Application Studio: The tool bridge connects ADT and BAS, which connects the front-end with the back-end development. It keeps the context of the services and entities as input for the Fiori generator.
  • The SAP Build Lobby as a single-entry point : You can start your ABAP development project from the lobby next to other build extension development options. You can access a list of ABAP systems directly from the lobby and keep a project reference to the ABAP system and development package.
  • The Graphical Modeler for ABAP: You can reuse CAP project models to generate ABAP Core Data Services (ABAP CDS) from it.   

In addition to all the benefits mentioned, it doesn’t matter if you’re building on-stack extensions in S/4HANA Cloud or side-by-side extensions on SAP BTP: SAP Build is the umbrella.

Picture 3: Extensibility Portfolio for S/4HANA public cloud edition

And this is only the beginning: We aim to integrate developer tools and capabilities into a seamless user centered offering and provide a clear developer tools strategy, so you can start building your SAP projects right away. Including ABAP Cloud in SAP Build is a testament to you as an ABAP Cloud developer. Your skills are more than important and strategic in the SAP application development and automation portfolio.

That is already exciting. And we haven’t even covered Joule’s capabilities in detail so far in this blog post.

We aim to increase the developer efficiency across the entire development lifecycle through generative AI and Joule embedded in all our IDEs and tools. Stay tuned for more news to come, especially on GenAI.

Resources

learn more about the topic:

  • Public Roadmap for SAP BTP, ABAP Environment
  • ABAP Cloud - Technical use cases and recommended technologies
  • Practicing Clean Core Extensibility For SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • SAP Certified Associate - Back-End Developer - ABAP Cloud
  • Extend SAP S/4HANA in the cloud and on premise with ABAP based extensions
  • ABAP Cloud FAQ
  • Building Low Code Extensions with Key User Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA and SAP Build
  • Extend SAP S/4HANA with SAP Build
  • Clean core extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: Shape the Future: Join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 3, 2024!
entry.content:
We're excited to announce that the third cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2024 has begun, featuring 33 new projects designed to enhance innovation and collaboration for our valued SAP customers and partners.

In today's rapidly evolving business environment, staying ahead is crucial. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative offers you a unique chance to gain early insights into SAP's product developments. By participating, you'll collaborate directly with our developers and product managers, influencing the future direction of our products.

Act quickly! Registration for the projects is open until November 15, 2024. Don’t miss this chance to make your voice heard and shape the next generation of SAP solutions.

To assist you in exploring the projects, we’ve created a detailed list for your convenience. Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the opportunities available:

SAP S/4HANA & SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Composable Enterprise: Reimagining Enterprise Software for Unprecedented Business Agility 
  • Help Where and When You Need It: User Assistance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Improve your Post Upgrade Tests(PUT) Experience - An Integrated and Smart approach to PUT
  • Insightful Analytics for Actual Costing
  • Joule applications to SAP S/4HANA Utilities
  • Monitoring End to End Master Data Replications in SAP Cloud ALM
  • New Cost Settlement for real estate scenarios (Service Charge Settlement / CAM)
  • Purpose as base for processing of S/4HANA Business Partner personal data – concept and migration”
  • Research Project: Production Scheduling using AI
  • Research: SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit - AI: Field Mapping Proposal
  • Using AI to Extract Data from the Delivery Note to Update the Inbound Delivery

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI Agent Builder - A Low-Code Innovation with SAP Build
  • AI-Powered Autonomous Sandbox
  • Cloud Security: Improving the Encryption Key Management Experience
  • Discover SAP Build Process Automation Triggers: Empowering Experts to Automate and Extend Use Cases
  • GenAI Content Provenance and Authenticity via Watermarking
  • Investigation into a Customer Security Dashboard
  • Knowledge Graph RAG on unstructured Data

SAP Concur

  • Concur Expense - India GST handling
  • Concur Expense – Integrating Sales Opportunity Data with Concur Travel and Expense Data
  • Concur Expense - Preventing overspend through cost control, spend planning, and regulatory
  • Concur Expense - SAP Integration with Concur Solutions: Employee- and Benefit- focused Budget Management
  • Concur Expense – The Future of Expense Administration
  • Concur Travel - Trip Management / New Trip List
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Manage and track carbon consumption in T&E spend

Customer Experience

  • Redesigned SAP Sales Cloud, add-in for Microsoft Outlook

Data & Analytics

  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud (XSC,XSA,CAP)

SAP Globalization

  • AI-supported Extension of Electronic Document Processing of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
  • Localization as a Self-Service for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with Business AI
  • Sales and Use Tax Reporting in United States - User Research

Lifecycle Management

  • Business Service Management in SAP Cloud ALM operations
  • Clean Core / RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard in SAP Cloud ALM

Others

  • Early Talent Management with SAP SuccessFactors

We believe that collaboration is essential for driving innovation. By partnering with our customers and partners, we can collectively shape the future of SAP’s products and revolutionize business operations.

So, mark your calendars, rally your team, and let’s embark on this journey together. Together, we can foster progress, and transform industries.

Don’t wait—register now at influence.sap.com and join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 3 in 2024!

Warm regards,

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 4 - Integration via Action
entry.content:
In this series of blogs, I will showcase how to integrate SAP AI Core services into SAP Build Apps to develop applications with AI capabilities.

  • Part 1: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Setup
  • Part 2: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via Javascript function
  • Part 3: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via REST API
  • Part 4: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via Action (current blog)

SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via Action

Currently, the recommended solution for integrating SAP AI Core service into your SAP Build Apps application is to use the new feature Action Integration. In this way, a SAP Build Action project (https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/create-actions-project ) for SAP AI Core service will be created first, and then integrated into your SAP Build Apps application.

1. Create an action in Lobby. and select "Build from Scratch." 

2. Add a custom action, select the operation as "POST," and provide the value of the endpoint according to your deployment of the AI core service, which you have setup in Part 1: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Setup

3. Add fields to Input Parameter and Body according the requirement of your SAP AI Core service instance

For examples:

sample JSON for input where you can get from https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-ai-core/sap-ai-core-service-guide/prompt-examples 

 

{
"messages": [
	{
		"role": "user",
		"content": "Your task is to generate a short summary of ..."
	}
],
"max_tokens": 100,
"temperature": 0.0,
"frequency_penalty": 0,
"presence_penalty": 0,
"stop": "null"
}

 

4. Test your SAP Build Action project

To test your SAP Build Action project, you need to add the BTP destination you created at Part 1: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Setup to SAP Build Control Tower. Still remember we set

 

sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled = true

 

in Part 1. This property makes the destination discoverable in the SAP Build Control Tower.

To understand more about how to manage destinations in Control Tower, please refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/manage-destinations

After adding the BTP destination, you can select it in your Action project and test it.

Once the test is successful, you can open the API response body and click "Generate Output" to populate your Action Output structure automatically. For more details, please refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/testing-actions Now, you have completed the creation of an SAP Build Action to consume the SAP AI Core service with just a copy and paste and a few mouse clicks.

5. Release and publish your action

6. Open your SAP Build Apps project, navigate to the Data tab, click "Add Integration," then select the "SAP Build Library" tile, browse, select the action you just published, and enable it in your SAP Build Apps project.

7. Switch back to "UI Canvas" tab, click the UI component, where you can want to trigger the action, open "Logic Canvas", drag and drop "Tigger Action" flow function to logic canvas and configure it

8. Please make sure to specify the correct BTP destination that you used when you created your action.

9. Configure the data binding for input and output of "Trigger Action" flow function

10. Preview and deploy your app on both web and mobile

To summarize what we have done, without writing a single line of code, just copy and paste, drag and drop, and a few mouse clicks, we created an SAP Build Action to consume SAP AI Core services, integrated it into SAP Build Apps, and developed an application with AI capabilities.

Compared to Part 2: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via Javascript function where you need to write JavaScript code, and  Part 3: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via REST API where you need to manually configure the resource schema for the REST API, the new solution with Action Integration offers a much better experience.

In future blogs, I will further discuss how to use Action Integration to enable more AI capabilities, such as RAG (Retrieval-augment generation), image analysis, anomaly detection, voice-to-text, etc. I will also write other blogs on how to use Action Integration to perform more complex data transactions, such as OData linking, deep inserts, etc. Stay tuned!

entry.title: Suncor Energy derives business value through SAP Business AI
entry.content:
The SAP MaxAttention CoE NA Team has been recognized as a prestigious SAP Innovation Awards 2024 Finalist! This accolade celebrates our innovative work with SUNCOR ENERGY, leveraging SAP Business AI to drive significant advancements. Our collaboration with Suncor exemplifies the power of cutting-edge technology in transforming business operations. Through SAP Business AI, we have delivered intelligent solutions that enhance efficiency, optimize processes, and foster sustainable growth. CIO Magazine has published an article featuring this success story.

Business Challenges

Suncor Energy has a legal and regulatory obligation to charge tax on intercompany transactions. Currently, the intercompany journal entries do not have tax amounts. The challenge is to compute the tax amount based on tax code and/or tax jurisdiction and post the journal entry with the consolidated tax amount. The solution should reduce the risks associated with regulatory filing, minimize manual effort, and shorten the period-end closing time.

Solution

As part of its monthly closing process, Suncor needed to reconcile inter-company tax postings for journal entries, which involved significant manual effort. To streamline this task, Suncor collaborated with the SAP MaxAttention team to develop an automation solution. Within less than four weeks, a prototype was delivered, followed by a successful production deployment in under six weeks. A key highlight of the solution was its ability to leverage Suncor’s existing SAP investments, enhancing and optimizing the process to meet evolving business challenges with rapid time-to-value. The hyper-automation solution utilized SAP Business AI, SAP Build Process Automation, S/4HANA REST APIs, Fiori UI, and SAP HANA Cloud, resulting in a reduced TCO and improved ROI.

Outcomes: 

  • Transaction Volume – Savings of $1.176 Million Interest and Fines Exposure (annually)
  • 40 pairings with over 5K customer line items per execution
  • ​​The Inter-company Tax Automation project helped Suncor reduce the risks associated with regulatory filing, minimize manual effort, and shorten the period-end closing time.
  • Suncor end users are happy with the solution, and the adoption is very high due to the reduction in tedious manual intervention and data processing; end users are now able to focus on strategic tasks rather than mundane data validation activities.

For further details, please refer to: https://www.cio.com/article/3544650/suncor-energy-derives-business-value-through-business-ai-and-automation.html 

Contact Person:  Rohit Dwivedi, Kiran Kola

entry.title: Driving ABAP Innovation: Key highlights and future trends from SAP TechEd 2024
entry.content:
Dear Developer Community,

I am thrilled to share with you some of the inspiring ABAP highlights from SAP TechEd 2024. The sessions were packed with valuable information and insights that are sure to empower and motivate every ABAP developer out there.

If you haven't had a chance to watch the video SAP TechEd 2024 | ABAP Highlights, I highly recommend it as it provides a concise overview of the latest developments in ABAP and the incredible possibilities that lie ahead:

 

In this blog post, I would like to take the opportunity to acknowledge the incredible work of our talented speakers and reflect on the wealth of knowledge they shared.

ABAP Cloud in SAP Build

Alexander Rother’s, Björn Schulz’s and Andre Borchert’s session AD100 | Unlock SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability to elevate your extensions emphasized the immense potential of the SAP ecosystem. The inclusion of ABAP Cloud in the SAP Build family marks a significant leap forward, forming the world’s strongest alliance for customer and partner development. By embracing the fusion development principle and harnessing the power of Generative AI, developers can expect unparalleled productivity and unprecedented outcomes. This transformative collaboration paves the way for innovation and advancement, driving the SAP ecosystem to new heights.

This integration was also discussed in session AD105 – Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build by Anne Keller and Tim Back. They showed a demo of how to integrate your ABAP Cloud projects into the lobby of SAP Build solutions.

Clean Core Extensibility

The session DT200 | Clean Core extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition by Olga Dolinskaja and Thomas Fiedler provided essential guidance on transforming legacy development to ABAP Cloud and Clean Core by using classic SAP APIs for the 3-Tier Extensibility Model. It also captured the upcoming S/4HANA Joule Agent in ABAP Development Tools (ADT) and how it will boost the development efficiency and create exciting possibilities for the future.

The 3-Tier Extensibility Model and how it serves to transform classic ABAP custom code into Clean Core compliant custom, was also highlighted in session DT100 | Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future by Carine Tchoutouo Djomo and Jan Gilg. By integrating of Generative AI across the entire SAP development lifecycle, they showcased the limitless possibilities for future innovation.

In session AD106 | Clean Core extensibility of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with ABAP Cloud, Volker Drees provided valuable insights into the Clean Core extensibility of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with ABAP Cloud. The overview of ABAP Cloud usage and the 3-Tier Extensibility Model, coupled with the integration of classic APIs (BAPIs) following the Clean Core principles, presented a roadmap for developers to create extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. 

Andre Fischer's session DT280 | Build Clean Core extensions with ABAP Cloud for SAP S/4HANA Cloud demonstrated the power of the new generators in ADT for building Clean Core extensions with ABAP Cloud for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. The session showed how easy it is to use the Field Extension Wizard and the UI Services Wizard to create extensions for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, empowering developers to unleash their creativity and take their applications to the next level.

Attendees of session DT201 | Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, presented by Alda Dollani and Thomas Schneider, gained valuable insights into finding the right extensibility tool for various use cases and identifying the right API or extension point. The session also showcased a cutting-edge extensibility wizard and AI capability, which is sure to revolutionize the way attendees approach their extensibility challenges.

ABAP AI

In the session AD107 | Boost your coding efficiency: Explore Joule’s ABAP developer capabilities, Jasmin Gruschke and Sebastian Baskovich focused on SAP's development of AI assistant tools for ABAP developers, with an exciting live demo and lab preview of Joule’s ABAP developer capabilities. ABAP AI truly is a game changer.

Carine Tchoutouo Djomo's sessions AD180 | Build extensions with ABAP Cloud and Joule's ABAP developer capabilities was an inspiring demonstration of the potential to accelerate application development using Joule ABAP developer capabilities. In her session, Carine demonstrated the implementation of business events with ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) for loosely coupled on-stack and side-by-side integration scenarios, as well as the display of hierarchical business data in SAP Fiori apps using Treeview.

In their session AI104 – Revolutionize ABAP development with Joule, our generative AI copilot Salma Sohrabi-Jahromi and Jens Weiler elaborated further on Joule's capabilities in ABAP development and how Joule can enrich everyday work.

Let me end this blog post with another video about the key TechEd Keynote messages about ABAP:

 

My heartfelt thanks to all the speakers and session owners for their invaluable contributions and insights.
All the best,

Sonja Liénard
SVP BTP ABAP

 

More to read

We recently published a blog post entitled The next step of ABAPs evolution: ABAP Cloud now part of SAP Build, which focuses on ABAP Cloud and its integration with SAP Build. I encourage you to read it if you haven't already.

On behalf of the entire ABAP community, I would also like to thank Michael Ameling for his continuous support and dedication to spreading the amazing ABAP news at TechEd 2024. His contributions have been invaluable, and we are incredibly grateful for his efforts. Check out his latest community blog post Unveiling today: SAP Build meets ABAP Cloud!

entry.title: SAP Developer News, October 24th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/10/24/sap-developer-news-october-24th-2024/

Description

SAP Build Apps now supports Actions!:

  • Chatbot Development Made Easy with SAP AI Core and Build Apps: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mE7nHx9XHAY&ab_channel=SAPDevelopers
  • Blog Post by Qui Shi: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-apps-integration-with-sap-ai-core-services-part-4-integration-via/ba-p/13885101
  • Official feature documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/actions

Devtoberfest Wrap Up

  • Devtoberfest Homepage: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest Event Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d

SAP TechEd On Tour

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • ASUG Tech Connect 2024: https://events.asug.com/event/9a5f27ca-d742-45bb-85e7-a6597b952900/summary?RefId=sap-splash-page-home&utm_campaign=ATC_24&utm_medium=sap&utm_source=referral&utm_term=sap-splash-page-home
  • SAPinsider EMEA 2024: https://reg.eventmobi.com/emea-2024?utm_source=marketing&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=teched&utm_content=sapmarketing
  • SAUG-x 2024: https://www.saug.com.au/WhatsOn/SAUG-x2024
  • UKISUG Connect 2024: https://www.ukisugconnect.co.uk/techx-at-connect?utm_source=TechEd_Webpage&utm_medium=Website&utm_campaign=TECHx_at_Connect
  • DSAG-TechXchange 2025: https://dsag.de/neuigkeiten/sap-teched-goes-dsag-techxchange-english/

ABAPconf 2024

  • Event Site:  https://abapconf.org/abapconf2024/

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 SAP Build Apps now supports Actions!
1:26 Devtoberfest Wrap Up
3:45 TechEd On Tour
4:49 ABAPConf 2024

Transcription

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for October 24th, 2024.

[Shrini] I hope you got a chance to watch this Devtoberfest session we did earlier this week on chatbot development made easy. If you haven't watched it, go ahead and watch the recording as I am using the same project to show you the newly added actions feature that is now available in SAP Build Apps. In the demo, I showed you how to create a data entity to connect to the Generative AI Hub and now with this feature, you could replace the data entity with an action project, configure the input, test, and save the output fields. Remember to add SAP Application Development Actions enabled to true to the destination in order for it to appear in the access project. After you release and publish the access to the library, you can enable it from the integrations tab. Now, there is also a trigger actions function available in the logic canvas and you could trigger this action while providing the BTP destination to get a response from the backend system. The response will now be available within outputs trigger action. Go ahead and try out the new actions feature in SAP Build Apps now, and you could replace a data entity in one of your build apps project with this new actions feature. You could refer to this blog post by Chu Shi, which has step by step instructions.

[Thomas] By the time you're seeing this new segment, our Devtoberfest for 2024 will have probably nearly come to a close. It's hard to imagine but this is the fifth year for Devtoberfest. I know it seems to us like just the other day we were planning our very first Devtoberfest and here we've grown to be this major part of your overall SAP TechEd experience. I'm really enjoying where we're at with Devtoberfest and TechEd. You know with TechEd you get the big announcements; you get introduction to new topics and then you can attend Devtoberfest session sessions to get a deeper dive into those same topics. It also allows us to cover a variety of maybe older topics, but ones that are still relevant to customers and partners out in the SAP ecosystem. So I'm really happy with the state where we are with Devtoberfest. It's great that we had over 60,000 unique people join and attend Devtoberfest sessions. We had tons of people, you know, almost 4,000 people enter the contest. We'll be announcing winners of the Devtoberfest contest soon. If all the validation of winners happens in enough time, we plan to announce those at the ASUG Tech Connect event, one of the SAP TechEd On Tour stops.

Speaking of SAP TechEd On Tour, that's really the next stage of continuing your TechEd journey. journey. If a Tekken on Tour stop is coming to an area near you, I encourage you to sign up and join to get that in-person experience with many of these new announced technologies. If it's not able to come your way, then we have lots of Code Jams planned for you next year, or if one's not coming to an area near you, consider hosting and request one to come to your area. We have plans to expand the parts of the world that we cover with Code Jams next year, hopefully making it to a few locations that we haven't been to in over four or five years. So with that, thank you, developer community, for another successful Devtoberfest, and we look forward to seeing you again in 2025.

[DJ] In this video I'm going to talk about innovating cutting-edge tech from the high seas. He could have kept his anchors away, but he didn't. Right. Tech out. I'll be coming up hot for a minute.

[Rich] Hey folks, Rich Heilman here. I hope everyone is having fun with the last week of Devtoberfest. But today, I wanted to make sure that you all know about the ABAP conference, which will take place on December 5th, 2024. This is a fantastic community-driven event specifically for ABAP developers, which will be streamed online. But you can also enter a drawing for a ticket to one of the on-site venues. The content is delivered in both German and English, depending on the session, and there are a ton of familiar names on the agenda. Way too many to mention here, so head over to the abopconference.org website for more information and details around the agenda.
